---
title: "Resources"
format: html
toc: true
bibliography: references.yaml
nocite: |
  @bollen1989, @bollen2026, @kline2023, @brown2015
---

## Reporting

When you report SEM results, you may want to reference these guidelines and examples:

- [JARS-Quant](https://apastyle.apa.org/jars/quantitative), also @appelbaum2018
    - The APA reporting standard for quantitative studies, including SEM.
- [JARS-Quant Table 7](https://apastyle.apa.org/jars/quant-table-7.pdf)
    - Specific standards for reporting SEM results, including data description, specification, estimation, model fit, and respecification.
- Brown (2015, Appendix 4.3)
    - Example of reporting a two-factor CFA.
- Kline (2023, Chapter 3)
    - See the *Reporting Standards* section for details and an example, and Table 3.1.

## Further reading

Beyond the workshop, these books are especially useful for SEM and related topics:

- Bollen, K. A. (1989). Structural equations with latent variables. Wiley. <https://doi.org/10.1002/9781118619179>
    - Also Bollen, K. A. (2026). Elements of structural equation models (SEMs) (1st ed.). Cambridge University Press. <https://doi.org/10.1017/9781009312806>
    - Foundational SEM references for the theory and practice of latent variable modeling.
- Kline, R. B. (2023). Principles and practice of structural equation modeling (5th ed.). Guilford.
    - Probably the most widely used SEM textbook, with clear explanations and practical examples.
- Brown, T. A. (2015). Confirmatory factor analysis for applied research (2nd ed.). The Guilford Press.
    - A clear applied introduction to CFA and measurement models.

## Free Online Materials

These are free, high-quality references you can keep using after the workshop.

- [lavaan Project Page](https://lavaan.ugent.be/)
    - The official site for `lavaan`, including excellent documentation and tutorials.
- [semTools wiki](https://github.com/simsem/semTools/wiki)
    - Helpful extensions for reliability, invariance testing, and related SEM tasks.
- [SEM videos by CenterStat](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=wHFrgp3SQMI&list=PLQGe6zcSJT0XCCmKAxqiA8AGl-z6HYn5B)
    - Nice set of videos covering SEM basics.
